reference: What brings you in here today? Hi, hi there I've been having this knee pain. It started almost immediately after I was I was playing basketball on the high school team and it was during practice I jumped up for a rebound and I, when I landed I heard a pop of my my left knee and I immediately felt pain. I just went off to the off to the sideline. OK, and when did this happen? Uh, this happened about. Probably like 6 hours ago. Maybe like a few hours ago. OK, OK, I see and since then have you been able to walk at all or have you been just, has it been very difficult? At first I was able to kind of limp off the court, but since then I I haven't really been able to walk. It's gotten pretty swollen since then and it looks bruised as well. OK, it's swollen and bruised in what kind of pain are you having, is it like sharp or is it aching? It's pretty sharp. It feels like there's people just stabbing me in the knee. OK. Uhm And um, do you have any, so you're having pain in your knee. Has it gone worse than when it started? Um. Yeah, it's yeah it feels a bit more painful than it did earlier. I think maybe the adrenaline was pumping a little bit. But yeah, now it's it's pretty pretty painful. OK OK uhm. And how much would you rate the pain out of 10, 10 being the worst pain you've felt in your life? Oh, I'd say this is like a 9 or a 10, this is it's really painful. OK, are you having pain anywhere else or is it just your left knee? Just my left knee. OK alright. Are you having other symptoms like fainting, dizziness? No, I I haven't had anything like that. OK, and are you having any fevers, chills or anything like that? No fevers or chills. OK. Any kind of numbness or tingling at all? No no Mr tingling. OK. Is the pain moving anywhere else from your knees? Uh, no I just feel like pretty like just right in my in my knee. OK, and what kind of motions are hurting you right now the most? I would say just like bending my knee, like, yeah, flexing my knee up as well as, like if I try to put any weight on it like I can't walk on it right now, I can't put any weight on my knee right now. And also even straightening it out hurts as well if I'm really flexing the muscle, so just trying to stay pretty immobile right now. OK, I see. OK, and in terms of other symptoms, any kind of chest pain, shortness of breath? No, nothing like that. OK, any rashes or skin changes? No, the only skin change that I that I have is the like bruising or looks like you like blood around my knee. OK you see is it warm to touch? Um. No, no, it's not warm. OK. In terms of your past medical history, any conditions you've been diagnosed with in the past? No, I've been healthy. OK, any medications that you take regularly? No, I don't. I don't think anything regularly. OK, any allergies to any medications? No, I don't. OK, any hospitalizations or previous surgeries? No, nothing like that. OK, and any family history of any musculoskeletal or rheumatologic conditions? Um, no no, nothing like that I I I mean I think my dad I had a rotator cuff injury or something like that but I mean he's in his 50s now so I think that happened at work or something I don't know. OK. Uhm, OK, and other than that, do you, so are you at school right now? I am yeah, I'm in grade grade 10 and playing well playing on the the high school basketball team and that's how I got this injury here. And have you had a similar injury at all in the past? OK and. Currently, right now, what's your living situation like? So I live with both of my parents and I also have a younger sibling. A sister, she's 8 years old and we have a dog as well. OK, uhm. Alright, and have you experimented at all with any recreational drugs, alcohol, or smoking cigarettes? No, nothing like that. OK, no, yeah, that's really that's really good that you're thinking like that. Any issues at school like bullying or do you feel unsafe either at home, around your home environment or at school? No, I don't feel unsafe. I mean, I know there's always those kids at school who are mean to others. But I mean, I, I generally feel safe. Yeah it it can, uh, straighten it out all like most of the way, it seems like it's bent a little still maybe from like the swelling and only I can't straighten it out the full way. I guess I can like see or on my knee I guess like the like a big bruise. OK, I see. OK. And. Yeah, so those are kind of like all the questions I had for you. It seems that based on the description of how the injury happened an the mechanism and what you're feeling right now and in terms of the examination, it's possible that you had knee ligament injury. Most likely in this case, the anterior cruciate ligament, but just to make sure we will need to do an MRI of your knee and see which ligament is affected and to what extent. Uh, and usually with the with the depending on what we find on the MRI sometimes it can be just conservative management, but however oftentimes you do require surgery, so it would be. Just a surgery where we put kind of like a scope in into your knee and through the scope we try to fix the ligament. So yeah first, we'll just do the MRI and see to what extent your knee injury is and how how affected the ligaments are, and then we'll go from there. Does that sound OK? It does. Yeah yeah, thank you. Welcome alright, Take care.
